journal_entry.description: Receipt of security deposit for building lease
journal_entry.rationale: Security deposit received is recorded as a liability (refundable deposit) because Mondelez has an obligation to return it to the tenant at lease end, subject to any damages or unpaid rent. The deposit does not represent earned revenue. Cash increases (asset debit), and Security Deposit Liability increases (liability credit). No revenue is recognized at this time because the deposit is refundable. The monthly rent of $10,000 is noted but not recorded until earned in future periods. No tax applies to security deposits as they are not consideration for a taxable supply.

output.reason: Cash received of $25,000 = 1 asset increase (Cash). The security deposit creates a liability (refundable deposit) on the credit side, but on the debit side we only record the cash receipt as an asset increase.

debit_classifier.reason: Cash received of $25,000 = 1 asset increase (Cash). The security deposit creates a liability (refundable deposit) on the credit side, but on the debit side we only record the cash receipt as an asset increase.

output.reason: The transaction involves receiving $25,000 cash as a security deposit. On the credit side: (1) Security deposit received creates a liability (refundable deposit payable to tenant) = liability increase. No revenue is recognized yet because the security deposit is refundable and not earned income - it represents an obligation to return the funds to the tenant at lease end.

credit_classifier.reason: The transaction involves receiving $25,000 cash as a security deposit. On the credit side: (1) Security deposit received creates a liability (refundable deposit payable to tenant) = liability increase. No revenue is recognized yet because the security deposit is refundable and not earned income - it represents an obligation to return the funds to the tenant at lease end.

output.reason: Entry correctly records receipt of security deposit. Balance verified: $25,000 debit = $25,000 credit. Cash (asset) correctly debited, Security Deposit Liability correctly credited. Under IFRS, security deposits are refundable obligations and properly classified as liabilities, not revenue. Amount matches transaction ($25,000). No tax applies to security deposits as they are not consideration for a taxable supply. Monthly rent of $10,000 is correctly excluded as it will be recognized when earned in future periods. All aspects correct.
